free_text: Mass distribution in 12-C induced fission of 232-Th
Radiochemical Division
BARC - TIFR 14UD Pelletron accelerator
Self-supporting thorium metal foils of a
thickness of approximately 2.0 mg/cm2 were used for
the irradiations. Super purity aluminum catcher foil
of a thickness of 6.8 mg/cm2 was used to collect the
fission products in the forward direction.
Carbon-12 beam. The current on the target was in
the range of 50 to 100 nA.
Irradiation time was 30 min. for short lived
fission products and 5 hours for long lived fission
products.
Activities of the short irradiation samples
were followed for about three days and those of long
irradiation for about three months.
No information on source of uncertainties
Correction for the fission products emitted in the
backward direction was made.
Tbl.1
Correction in reference was made, according
to M.Bossant remark

In order to arrive at the mass yields from
the measured independent and cumulative cross sections
of a given fission product, it is necessary to know
the charge distribution of the mass chain, to correct
for the contribution of the unobserved members of mass
chain. In the present work the unchanged charge
distribution hypothesis was used to obtain the centroid
of the charge distribution.
Deduced from Ba-141 yield
Deduced from Ce-141 yield
